name: Vía Verde del Campo de Cartagena y Vía Verde Mazarrón — in gravel from Totana
name_original: Vía Verde del Campo de Cartagena y Vía Verde Mazarrón — en gravel desde Totana
region_intro: Touring cycling around Totana offers diverse landscapes, from the rugged, pine-covered mountains of Sierra Espuña Natural Park to fertile valleys with orange and lemon orchards. The region features varied terrain, including paved roads, unpaved trails, and disused railway lines converted into Vías Verdes. Cyclists can navigate challenging climbs to viewpoints like Collado Bermejo or explore unique geological formations such as the Badlands of Gebas. Best touring cycling routes around Totana The most popular touring cycling route is Vía Verde del Campo de Cartagena y Vía Verde Mazarrón — in gravel from Totana, a 31.0 miles (49.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 25 minutes to complete, offering a generally flat, earthy surface suitable for gravel biking. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Plaza de la Balsa Vieja – Collado Bermejo Gravel Descent loop from Totana, a difficult 30.0 miles (48.4 km) path that includes a rewarding climb to a scenic pass within Sierra Espuña. Local touring cyclists also love the Plaza de la Balsa Vieja loop from Totana, a 26.0 miles (41.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 13 minutes. Touring cycling around Totana is defined by the pine-covered mountains of Sierra Espuña, extensive Vías Verdes, and fragrant orange and lemon orchards. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy greenways to demanding mountain climbs. The routes in Totana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 900 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Totana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Totana?
A: There are over 70 touring cycling routes around Totana listed on komoot, offering a wide range of experiences. These include routes through the Sierra Espuña Natural Park, along Vías Verdes, and through fragrant orchards.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Totana?
A: Touring cycling around Totana offers diverse terrain. You'll find everything from smooth, paved roads in the Sierra Espuña Natural Park to unpaved trails suitable for gravel biking, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017252684/via-verde-del-campo-de-cartagena-y-via-verde-mazarron-in-gravel-from-totana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Campo de Cartagena y Vía Verde Mazarrón&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features unique geological formations like the dramatic Badlands of Gebas and scenic paths through orange and lemon orchards.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Totana?
A: The Murcia region, including Totana, generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly ideal, with milder temperatures and less intense sun, making it comfortable for longer rides. Summers can be very hot, especially in the middle of the day, while winters are generally mild but can have cooler mornings.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Totana?
A: Yes, Totana offers routes suitable for various skill levels. For beginners and families, the Vías Verdes (greenways) are an excellent choice. These disused railway lines, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017252684/via-verde-del-campo-de-cartagena-y-via-verde-mazarron-in-gravel-from-totana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Campo de Cartagena y Vía Verde Mazarrón&lt;/a&gt;, typically offer generally flat, earthy surfaces, making them accessible and enjoyable for all. There are 2 easy and 26 moderate routes available.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while cycling around Totana?
A: Many routes will take you through the stunning Sierra Espuña Natural Park, known for its pine-covered mountains. You can also visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/705186" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Eulalia&lt;/a&gt;, or cycle past the unique Badlands of Gebas. The region is also dotted with fragrant orange and lemon orchards. For panoramic views, consider routes that pass through high points like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2953991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado Bermejo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Totana?
A: Yes, there are several circular touring cycling routes available. For example, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19382279" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de la Balsa Vieja loop from Totana&lt;/a&gt; takes you through varied terrain, while the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14624892" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de la Balsa Vieja – Tajo-Segura Canal Path loop from Totana&lt;/a&gt; offers a different experience. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40365240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Airplane Viewpoint loop from Venta la Rata&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find routes that offer great viewpoints in Totana?
A: Absolutely. The Sierra Espuña Natural Park is rich with scenic viewpoints. A notable one is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2953991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado Bermejo&lt;/a&gt;, located at 1,201 meters, which offers breathtaking vistas of the surrounding mountains and the Espuña River Valley. The difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10321852" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de la Balsa Vieja – Collado Bermejo Gravel Descent loop from Totana&lt;/a&gt; specifically includes this rewarding climb.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Totana?
A: The touring cycling routes around Totana are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from the quiet, smooth roads of Sierra Espuña to the unique Vías Verdes, and the challenging climbs that offer spectacular views.

Q: Is it possible to cycle with dogs on the touring routes in Totana?
A: While many natural areas in Spain are generally dog-friendly, specific regulations can vary, especially within natural parks like Sierra Espuña. It's advisable to check local park rules regarding dogs, particularly concerning leashing and waste disposal, to ensure a pleasant experience for everyone. Vías Verdes are often more accommodating for dogs on a leash.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Totana?
A: Totana itself offers various parking options within the town center, such as near Plaza de la Balsa Vieja, which serves as a common starting point for many routes. When heading into Sierra Espuña, there are designated parking areas at visitor centers and trailheads. It's recommended to check specific route descriptions on komoot for suggested starting points with parking facilities.

Q: Are there options for public transport to access touring cycling routes in Totana?
A: Totana is connected by regional bus services, which can be used to reach the town. However, direct public transport options to specific trailheads within Sierra Espuña Natural Park are limited. Cyclists typically start their tours from Totana town center or use personal vehicles to reach more remote starting points. Some Vías Verdes are accessible directly from the town.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or accommodation along the routes?
A: Totana town center offers numerous cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. Within Sierra Espuña, the former monastery at the Sanctuary of Santa Eulalia has been converted into a hotel with a café, providing a convenient stop. The Ricardo Codorníu Visitors Centre also offers facilities. For longer routes, it's wise to carry sufficient water and snacks, as services can be sparse in more remote areas of the natural park.
tips: Beautiful pictures while we walk the Vía Verde del Campo de Cartagena between Totana and Cartagena
Heaped up old railway line from Mazarron to Cartagena, a dream for fast graveling

At this point we are in the heart of Totana. The Murcian municipality is located in the Bajo Guadalentín region. You can visit the church of Santiago el Mayor or the archaeological sites. La Bastida de Totana is one of the most important in continental Europe from the Bronze Age located on a hill on the outskirts of the city.
